introduction: In this presentation, we will explore how our IT Director, Jason Wong, implemented internal cyber competitions in May 2025, and the resultant effects on our company's cybersecurity posture. Given our increasing reliance on digital platforms, it's crucial to understand the importance of such initiatives.

subsections.heading: Cybersecurity Landscape at Lee's Market
subsections.content:
• Lee's Market follows a stringent cybersecurity protocol to protect sensitive customer and business data
• In 2023, a new firewall system was introduced to enhance network security
• Regular audits are conducted to ensure compliance with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
• Lee's Market has a dedicated cybersecurity team that monitors potential threats 24/7
• The cybersecurity team has successfully mitigated over 500 potential data breaches in 2024
• Introduced a new password management system in 2024 to increase employee access security
subsections.justification: This slide discusses general cybersecurity measures at Lee's Market but does not mention internal cyber competitions or Jason Wong's role.

subsections.heading: Overview of Internal Cyber Competitions
subsections.content:
• Internal cyber competitions aim to educate employees about potential cyber threats
• The format of the competition often involves a Capture The Flag (CTF) model
• Employees from all departments are encouraged to participate in the competition
• The competitions are designed to be engaging and fun, while also being educational
• Rewards are often provided to the winners of these competitions
• The competitions are held periodically throughout the year
• The initiative was first introduced in early 2022.
subsections.justification: This content overviews the competitions without mentioning Jason Wong or the May 2025 event.

subsections.heading: Impact of Cyber Competitions on Security
subsections.content:
• The competitions have increased awareness about cyber threats among employees
• There has been a 20% reduction in successful phishing attacks since the introduction of the competitions
• Employees have become more vigilant in identifying and reporting potential risks
• The competitions have fostered a culture of security within the organization
• Improved employee morale and team spirit have been observed as an added benefit.
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the impacts of the competitions but does not mention Jason Wong or the specific impact of the May 2025 event.

conclusion: Our internal cyber competitions, facilitated by Jason Wong, have significantly enhanced our cybersecurity capabilities. Moving forward, we should consider expanding these initiatives to maintain our robust defense against potential threats.

image_queries.slide_title: Impact of Cyber Competitions on Security
